content: Use the discriminant formula D = b^2 - 4ac. For D > 0 there are two real roots, for D = 0 one real root, for D < 0 no real roots over the reals.
Common derivative rules: (x^n)' = n*x^(n-1), (sin x)' = cos x, (cos x)' = -sin x, product rule and chain rule are frequently needed.
Useful facts: sum of angles in a triangle is 180 degrees, Pythagorean theorem for right triangles, area formulas include 1/2 * base * height.
Systems of linear equations can be solved by substitution, elimination, or matrix methods. Check that the final solution satisfies all equations.
